title: gluta glabra
intro: Gluta glabra, also known as the Nyatoh tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is a deciduous tree valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains compounds such as gallic acid and tannins. The medicinal properties of Gluta glabra include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of various infections. Gluta glabra is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, and can be found in traditional medicine practices in Southeast Asia.
